text:
The moon cast a pallor over everything. It's no wonder, either; all around me lay the aftermath of a terrible storm. The wind, relentless, had howled through the forest all night. Small birds, once filled with song, now perched motionlessly, their eyes distant. I wandered along a narrow path I'd often taken alone; I needed some quiet time, reflective.

There's a clearing, small and sheltered. In its center, nestled against a towering oak, was a patch of old rose petals. They hadn't all fallen there; something had gathered them – sweet buds, perhaps. I knelt down to tease a few loose petals from their stem.

I breathed deeply, feeling the tranquil space. The quiet spoke of secrets, of time spent here centuries ago. A gentle breeze rustled the leaves beside me, and I looked up to see a solitary figure crossing the clearing.

Her hair was like firelight, streaming golden waves down her back. She carried herself
